name: Yoshihiko Noda
bio: Yoshihiko Noda served as the Prime Minister of Japan from 2011 to 2012, leading the country during the post-Fukushima recovery period.
_evidence: As Prime Minister, he made the final official announcement and government decision to authorize the restart of the Ohi units.

name: Issei Nishikawa
bio: Issei Nishikawa served as the Governor of Fukui Prefecture from 2003 to 2019, overseeing the region that hosts many of Japan's nuclear power plants.
_evidence: His approval was a critical prerequisite for the central government to proceed with the restart of the reactors located in his prefecture.

name: Shinobu Tokioka
bio: Shinobu Tokioka served as the mayor of Ohi, a town in Fukui Prefecture heavily economically dependent on the local nuclear power plant.
_evidence: As the mayor of the host municipality, his local consent was a necessary condition for the restart of the Ohi units.
